mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals rapid labor market normalization, which boosts consumer confidence and aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in residential housing starts and a sharp drop in revolving consumer credit indicate a synchronized demand shock that erodes corporate revenue growth expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 500 and RSAFS declines > 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 60% of historical episodes where both proxies breach the specified thresholds simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the industrial production index reflects accelerating manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-month Spearman correlation between INDPRO momentum and 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.10 across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP exceeding 2.0% annualized rate confirms robust economic expansion, which sustains earnings revisions upward and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns following GDP growth > 2.0% are statistically indistinguishable from baseline returns (p-value > 0.10 in a paired t-test).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.2% triggers inflation hedging flows and anticipates tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered months over a 10-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly payroll growth exceeding 100,000 jobs and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which accelerates wage-driven consumption and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint indicator fails to outperform a random baseline in predicting 5-day returns in at least 40% of out-of-sample testing windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 500,000 units annually combined with real GDP growth above trend signals a housing-led economic recovery, which lifts financial sector earnings and construction-related cyclicals, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if 60-day forward returns following the trigger condition are negative or flat in more than 55% of historical instances.
